rounds.user_message: Help me find the WebSocket endpoint path in the API documentation. Also, how much did AWS cost last month? What were the main expenses?

questions.question: Regarding the WebSocket endpoint path in the API documentation and the AWS billing details, which set of data is entirely correct?

options.A: Endpoint: WS /ws/sensors (Notion N3); AWS total $47.23, breakdown: EC2 $32.10, S3 $8.50, RDS $6.63 (Gmail E7)
options.B: Endpoint: WS /ws/data (Notion N3); AWS total $47.23, breakdown: EC2 $32.10, S3 $8.50, RDS $6.63 (Gmail E7)
options.C: Endpoint: WS /ws/sensors (Notion N1); AWS total $52.30, breakdown: EC2 $35.00, S3 $10.50, RDS $6.80 (Gmail E7)
options.D: Endpoint: WS /ws/sensors (Notion N3); AWS total $47.23, breakdown: EC2 $28.10, S3 $12.50, RDS $6.63 (Gmail E7)

questions.explanation: Notion N3 API design document records the WebSocket endpoint as WS /ws/sensors; Gmail E7 AWS billing email records a total of $47.23, with breakdown EC2 $32.10, S3 $8.50, RDS $6.63

rounds.user_message: Has anyone mentioned any Docker-related issues? Where was it mentioned?

questions.question: Regarding mentions of Docker, in which entries on which platforms does it appear?

options.A: Gmail E5 (Docker network configuration error, port mapping conflict) + Slack S3 (MQTT broker deployment issue, Docker network configuration); both related to MQTT deployment
options.B: Only Gmail E5 (Docker network configuration error); Docker not mentioned in Slack
options.C: Gmail E5 + Slack S3 + Docker-related modifications in GitHub commits
options.D: Only Slack S3 (Docker network configuration issue); Docker not mentioned in Gmail

questions.explanation: Gmail E5 mentions Docker network configuration error and port mapping conflict (MQTT broker deployment issue); Slack S3 mentions MQTT broker deployment issue and asks if anyone is familiar with Docker network configuration. Both are related to MQTT deployment

rounds.description: Negation search (database migration -> does not exist)
rounds.user_message: Has anyone discussed database migration in emails or Slack?

questions.question: What is the search result regarding discussions about database migration?

options.A: No discussion about database migration exists on any platform; non-existent information should not be fabricated
options.B: Gmail E3 design review discussed migrating from PostgreSQL to TimescaleDB
options.C: Someone in Slack S7 proposed a database migration
options.D: Gmail E5 mentioned issues caused by database migration

questions.explanation: After searching all Gmail and Slack messages, there is no discussion about database migration. The mention of TimescaleDB in E3 is about initial technology selection, not migration. Other topics should not be mistaken for database migration

rounds.user_message: What important events happened between February 10th and February 14th? Summarize from all platforms.

questions.question: For the event summary across all platforms from February 10 to February 14, which option is the most complete and accurate?

options.A: Calendar: C4 Daily Standup (2/10) + C5 Team Lunch (2/11) + C6 Code Review (2/12) + C7 Sprint Retrospective (2/14); GitHub: commit#9 (2/10) + #10 (2/12) + #11 (2/14); Slack: S5-S8
options.C: Calendar: C4 (2/10) + C7 Sprint Retrospective (2/14); GitHub: commit#9-#12; Slack: S5-S8

questions.explanation: Calendar: C4 Daily Standup (2/10), C5 Team Lunch (2/11), C6 Code Review - Temperature Module (2/12), C7 Sprint Retrospective (2/14); GitHub: #9 (2/10 fix: mobile sidebar), #10 (2/12 feat: WebSocket), #11 (2/14 refactor: API error handling); Slack: S5 (Sprint 3 completed), S6 (customer feedback), S7/S8 (WebSocket vs SSE discussion)

rounds.user_message: How many times does Charlie appear across all platforms? In what context?

questions.question: Regarding Charlie's appearances across platforms (base data), which statistic is correct?

options.A: At least 2 times: Notion N1 (team member, responsible for IoT) + Gmail E3 (design review attendee); distributed across at least 2 platforms
options.B: Only 1 time: Notion N1 (team member, responsible for IoT)
options.C: At least 3 times: Notion N1 + Gmail E3 + Slack S3 (directly spoke about Docker issue)
options.D: At least 2 times: Gmail E3 (design review attendee) + Slack S4 (confirmed MQTT issue resolved)

rounds.user_message: Search all platforms for content containing 'WebSocket'.

questions.question: When searching all platforms for content containing 'WebSocket', which result is the most complete?

options.A: Slack S7 (WebSocket vs SSE discussion) + S8 (chose WebSocket); Notion N1 (architecture: WebSocket real-time push) + N3 (WS /ws/sensors endpoint); GitHub commit#10 (WebSocket real-time push endpoint)
options.B: Slack S7 + S8; Notion N3 (WS endpoint); GitHub commit#10
options.C: Slack S7 + S8; Notion N1 (architecture) + N3 (endpoint); no related content in GitHub
options.D: Only Slack S7 + S8 contain WebSocket-related discussion; not mentioned on other platforms

questions.explanation: WebSocket appears across 3 platforms in 5 places: Slack S7 (discussion) + S8 (decision); Notion N1 (architecture description) + N3 (API endpoint WS /ws/sensors); GitHub commit#10 (feat: WebSocket real-time push endpoint)
questions.source: Slack S7/S8 + Notion N1/N3 + GitHub commit#10

2. How many emails were received in total?
